topic: Two colleagues are both considered for the same promotion
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elias watched the announcement scroll across the office monitor, his jaw tightening almost imperceptibly. Sarah had been the obvious choice, the one who had championed her projects in every meeting. When the VP finally called his top performers, Elias felt a strange, cold pressure settle in his chest. He stood up, the cheap plastic of his chair scraping loudly against the floor, and walked toward the water cooler. He picked up a bottle of water, his knuckles white against the cool plastic, and took a long, deliberate sip, trying to keep his expression neutral as he mentally cataloged every slight that had led to this outcome.

[story 2]

"Of course, Sarah gets it," Mark muttered under his breath as he gathered his files, the sound barely audible over the hum of the ventilation system. He felt a distinct clenching in his stomach, a tightness that made breathing shallow. He didn't look at Amelia, who was laughing easily with the department head. Mark kept his gaze fixed on a spot on the wall, focusing intently on a tiny scuff mark, his hands flexing at his sides. When Amelia finally approached, offering a polite, practiced smile, Mark managed a tight nod, the gesture feeling forced and brittle.

[story 3]

"Well, that's settled then," Clara said, her voice dripping with practiced cordiality as she handed Elias a printout. He took the paper, his fingers brushing hers—a contact that sent a jolt of unwelcome heat through him. He felt a sudden, sharp desire to retreat, to simply disappear into the beige cubicle walls. He forced his lips into a thin, unreadable line. Internally, a slow burn ignited; he stared at the corner of the desk, focusing on the grain of the wood, refusing to acknowledge the clear disparity between his own hard work and the perceived favoritism displayed.
stories: Elias watched the announcement scroll across the office monitor, his jaw tightening almost imperceptibly. Sarah had been the obvious choice, the one who had championed her projects in every meeting. When the VP finally called his top performers, Elias felt a strange, cold pressure settle in his chest. He stood up, the cheap plastic of his chair scraping loudly against the floor, and walked toward the water cooler. He picked up a bottle of water, his knuckles white against the cool plastic, and took a long, deliberate sip, trying to keep his expression neutral as he mentally cataloged every slight that had led to this outcome.

"Of course, Sarah gets it," Mark muttered under his breath as he gathered his files, the sound barely audible over the hum of the ventilation system. He felt a distinct clenching in his stomach, a tightness that made breathing shallow. He didn't look at Amelia, who was laughing easily with the department head. Mark kept his gaze fixed on a spot on the wall, focusing intently on a tiny scuff mark, his hands flexing at his sides. When Amelia finally approached, offering a polite, practiced smile, Mark managed a tight nod, the gesture feeling forced and brittle.

"Well, that's settled then," Clara said, her voice dripping with practiced cordiality as she handed Elias a printout. He took the paper, his fingers brushing hers—a contact that sent a jolt of unwelcome heat through him. He felt a sudden, sharp desire to retreat, to simply disappear into the beige cubicle walls. He forced his lips into a thin, unreadable line. Internally, a slow burn ignited; he stared at the corner of the desk, focusing on the grain of the wood, refusing to acknowledge the clear disparity between his own hard work and the perceived favoritism displayed.
